page: Amazon Web Services c8gb.2xlarge is a compute-optimized instance powered by the AWS Graviton processor family. Built on the ARM64 architecture, this instance features 8 dedicated physical cores using the ARMv9 Neoverse-V2 model running at 2.8 GHz. It is equipped with 16.0 GB of system memory, yielding a 2.0 GB memory-to-core ratio. The CPU configuration includes 64 KB of L1d cache, 2 MB of L2 cache, and 36 MB of L3 cache. The instance operates on the Nitro hypervisor and provides a baseline network bandwidth of 17 Gbps. It does not include local storage or dedicated GPU hardware.

Performance benchmarks indicate strong single-core capabilities, with single-threaded gzip compression reaching 30.28 GB/sec and single-threaded bzip3 decompression reaching 14.48 GB/sec. The Geekbench single-core score of 1890.0 and text processing score of 2337.0 also rank in the top tier. Multi-core and memory bandwidth metrics generally fall within the average range, including a Geekbench multi-core score of 10105.0 and a multi-threaded bzip3 decompression speed of 109.93 GB/sec. However, the instance exhibits weaker performance in cryptographic operations, with a Passmark CPU encryption score of 1506.69, and struggles with medium-sized LLM prompt processing, which registers at 15.52 tokens/sec.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the ARM64 architecture and dedicated core allocation provide a resource-dense profile for compute-bound workloads without the overhead of hyperthreading. The instance is well-suited for single-threaded processing tasks, text processing, and lightweight web serving. It is also appropriate for software compilation and database operations that fit within the 16.0 GB memory footprint, though workloads requiring heavy cryptographic acceleration or large-scale language model inference may encounter performance bottlenecks.
description: Amazon Web Services c8gb.2xlarge is a compute-optimized instance featuring 8 dedicated ARM64 physical cores powered by the AWS Graviton ARMv9 Neoverse-V2 processor running at 2.8 GHz. It is configured with 16.0 GB of memory, a 17 Gbps baseline network bandwidth, and operates on the Nitro hypervisor without local storage or GPU accelerators. Benchmark data highlights strong single-core performance, particularly in single-threaded gzip compression and text processing. Multi-core execution, software compilation, and memory bandwidth perform in the average tier, while cryptographic operations and medium-model LLM prompt processing represent notable performance tradeoffs. This instance is best suited for compute-bound workloads, single-threaded data processing, software development, and lightweight database operations that benefit from dedicated ARM64 cores and a 2.0 GB per core memory allocation.

tagline: An ARM64 compute-optimized instance featuring dedicated physical cores and strong single-threaded performance for text and data processing workloads.

16.0 GB of system memory providing a balanced 2.0 GB per core ratio
Strong single-core performance in text processing and single-threaded compression
